title: Is VoIP Worth It for a Small Business?
description: A practical look at when internet-based business phone service can reduce cost and improve flexibility.
quickAnswer: VoIP is often worth it when your team needs remote calling, voicemail-to-email, call routing, and lower per-user costs, but quality depends on internet reliability and provider setup.
bestFor: Teams with remote or hybrid staff
Businesses paying high legacy phone bills
Companies that need call routing or analytics
notBestFor: Organizations with strict on-premise phone requirements
Teams that only need one basic line

scenario.title: Example Cost Scenario
details: Potential savings depend on taxes, devices, and contract terms

faqs.question: Does VoIP require new phones?
faqs.answer: Not always. Some teams use existing compatible phones, adapters, or softphone apps.

faqs.question: Is VoIP call quality reliable?
faqs.answer: It can be reliable with adequate bandwidth, network quality, and proper configuration.
